verdict: 3 determinism leaks: validate-gate is a pure extraction prompt (high), merge-phase runs deterministic gates before judgment (medium), create-branch extracts phase metadata (medium)

findings.title: validate-gate capability is entirely deterministic extraction
findings.evidence: The entire prompt is: 'Read progress.md. Parse for: review-passed: true/false and tests-passed: true/false. Report each gate's status. If false, list the exact blocker from review-report.md or test-report.md.' Every instruction matches signal-verbs 'parse', 'validate', and 'list' with no judgment component — the model is doing regex extraction of boolean fields and structured file reads. Same input always yields same output (determinism test: yes; unit test: yes; meaning/context required: no).
findings.recommendation: Determinism leak — push to script. Write scripts/validate-gate.py that reads progress.md, extracts the two boolean fields via regex, optionally reads the blocker reports, and emits compact JSON ({phase, review_passed, tests_passed, blockers}). The prompt then consumes the JSON and makes the final merge-readiness judgment call, which is where the model's interpretation earns its place.

findings.title: merge-phase gate checks are deterministic pre-work before the merge judgment
findings.evidence: Three sequential gates before the merge decision: (1) 'Read phases-index.md to confirm this phase is next in sequence' — cross-referencing phase name against a file index, (2) 'Read progress.md and verify that review-passed: true', (3) 'verify that tests-passed: true'. Gates 1-3 are all deterministic: same files always produce the same ordering answer and boolean check. Only the subsequent squash-vs-merge-commit choice and conflict notification are judgment work.
findings.recommendation: Determinism leak — push gates to a script. A pre-pass script (shared with validate-gate) should read phases-index.md and progress.md and emit {phase_name, is_next_in_sequence, review_passed, tests_passed} as JSON. The prompt then imports this JSON and applies merge judgment (deciding merge strategy, handling conflicts) on top.

findings.title: create-branch extracts phase metadata from phases-index.md
findings.evidence: Instruction: 'Read phases-index.md at {project-root}/masb-workspace/phases-index.md to determine the phase number and whether this phase depends on another.' The model must parse a markdown file to extract structured fields (phase number N, dependencies). Same phases-index.md always yields the same metadata — this is extraction, not interpretation. The branch name template 'phase/N-nom-de-phase' is then assembled from the extracted data.
findings.recommendation: Determinism leak — push to pre-pass script. Write or reuse a script that reads phases-index.md, extracts each phase's number, name, and dependencies as JSON, and hands the model {phase_number, phase_name, depends_on, branch_name_candidate}. The model then applies judgment about branch naming edge cases or user overrides rather than doing raw extraction from markdown.
